revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Tanba_Michinushi-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''丹波道主命'''（たんばのみちのうしのみこと、生没年不詳）とは、記紀における[[皇族]]（王族）。『[[日本書紀]]』では丹波道主命、『[[古事記]]』では旦波比古多多須美知能宇斯王。[[四道将軍]]のひとりで、[[丹波]]に派遣されたとされる。[[三河]][[宝飯郡|穂]]別の祖。[[景行天皇]]の外祖父。
[[彦坐王]]（ひこいますのみこ）の子で、[[開化天皇]]の孫に当たる。母は息長水依比売娘（おきながのみずよりひめ。天之御影神の女）。 同母兄弟に、水穂之真若王（近淡海安直の祖）、神大根王（本巣国造・三野前国造の祖）、水穂五百依比売、御井津比売がいる。 一説に[[彦湯産隅命]]（ひこゆむすみのみこと、開化天皇の子）の子。
妻は、丹波之河上之摩須郎女（たんばのかわかみのますのいらつめ）。 子は[[日葉酢媛命]]（垂仁天皇皇后）、[[渟葉田瓊入媛]]（同妃）、[[真砥野媛]]（同妃）、[[薊瓊入媛]]（同妃）、[[竹野媛]]、[[朝廷別王]]。
京都府[[久美浜]]の地名は、道主命の伺帯した「国剣」から「国見」「久美」となり「くみのみなと」「くみの見谷」「くみの浜」など久美浜の地名の起源になったと言われる。

京都府[[久美浜]]の地名は、道主命の伺帯した「国剣」から「国見」「久美」となり「くみのみなと」「くみの見谷」「くみの浜」など久美浜の地名の起源になったと言われる。 [[Category:日本神話の人物|たんはのみちのうしのみこと]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族|たんはのみちのうしのみこと]]

revision.text:
'''Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto''' (丹波道主命, ''Tanba no Michinushi no Mikoto'' or ''Taniha no Michinushi no Mikoto'') is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族}} from ancient times as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto" or "Tanba Michinushi no Ō," while the ''Kojiki'' uses the name "Tanba Hikotatatsumichi no Ushi no Mikoto" (丹波比古多多須美知能宇斯王).{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
He is the grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}} (the 9th Emperor of Japan) and the maternal grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} (the 12th Emperor). As one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun}}, he is said to have been dispatched to the {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国}} to aid in its development and pacification.
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto is the grandson of Emperor Kaika. His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別}}, and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}}, he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sasayama, Hyōgo|ja|丹波篠山市}} is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}} enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun}}

The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto" or "Tanba Michinushi no Ō," while the ''Kojiki'' uses the name "Tanba Hikotatatsumichi no Ushi no Mikoto" (丹波比古多多須美知能宇斯王).{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
He is the grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft}} (the 9th Emperor of Japan) and the maternal grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}} (the 12th Emperor). As one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=draft}}, he is said to have been dispatched to the {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国|12=draft}} to aid in its development and pacification.
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto is the grandson of Emperor Kaika. His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命|12=draft}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|12=draft}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|12=draft}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft}}, he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Sasayama, Hyōgo|ja|丹波篠山市|12=draft}} is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}} enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=draft}}

His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命|12=draft}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|12=draft}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|12=draft}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft}}, he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Sasayama, Hyōgo|ja|丹波篠山市|12=draft}} is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}} enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto''' (丹波道主命, ''Tanba no Michinushi no Mikoto'' or ''Taniha no Michinushi no Mikoto'') is a legendary {{ill|Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=draft}} from ancient times as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}.
The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto" or "Tanba Michinushi no Ō," while the ''Kojiki'' uses the name "Tanba Hikotatatsumichi no Ushi no Mikoto" (丹波比古多多須美知能宇斯王).{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
He is the grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft}} (the 9th Emperor of Japan) and the maternal grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}} (the 12th Emperor). As one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=draft}}, he is said to have been dispatched to the {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波国|12=draft}} to aid in its development and pacification.
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto is the grandson of Emperor Kaika. His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命|12=draft}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|12=draft}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|12=draft}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft}}, he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Sasayama, Hyōgo|ja|丹波篠山市|12=draft}} is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}} enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=draft}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:道主命 Michinushi no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|qid=Q2658252}}|spouse={{ill|Q109930105|qid=Q109930105}}|children={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|qid=Q8375821}}; {{ill|Q106114160|qid=Q106114160}}; {{ill|Q108043975|qid=Q108043975}}; {{ill|Q109930164|qid=Q109930164}}; {{ill|Q109930863|qid=Q109930863}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|qid=Q11487910}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|qid=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|qid=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Q11626514|qid=Q11626514}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|qid=Q11466268}}; {{ill|Ōtsutsukitarune no Miko|qid=Q97539311}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11368587]] Wikidata name: Tanba no Michinushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto''' (丹波道主命, ''Tanba no Michinushi no Mikoto'' or ''Taniha no Michinushi no Mikoto'') is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from ancient times as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto" or "Tanba Michinushi no Ō," while the ''Kojiki'' uses the name "Tanba Hikotatatsumichi no Ushi no Mikoto" (丹波比古多多須美知能宇斯王).{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
He is the grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] (the 9th Emperor of Japan) and the maternal grandfather of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (the 12th Emperor). As one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, he is said to have been dispatched to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] to aid in its development and pacification.
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto is the grandson of Emperor Kaika. His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoku clans of Shiga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni clans of Mikawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 穂国造|1=Hokuni clans of Mikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Uchino Sukune|qq=|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}}, and [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumobe Kofun|qq=|1=Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215205}} in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Sasayama, Hyōgo]] is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]] enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}

The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoku clans of Shiga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni clans of Mikawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 穂国造|1=Hokuni clans of Mikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Uchino Sukune|qq=|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}}, and [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumobe Kofun|qq=|1=Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215205}} in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Sasayama, Hyōgo]] is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]] enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:道主命 Michinushi no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|spouse={{ill|Q109930105|WD=Q109930105}}|children={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|WD=Q8375821}}; {{ill|Q106114160|WD=Q106114160}}; {{ill|Q108043975|WD=Q108043975}}; {{ill|Q109930164|WD=Q109930164}}; {{ill|Q109930863|WD=Q109930863}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Q11626514|WD=Q11626514}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|WD=Q11466268}}; {{ill|Ōtsutsukitarune no Miko|WD=Q97539311}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11368587]] Wikidata name: Tanba no Michinushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto''' (丹波道主命, ''Tanba no Michinushi no Mikoto'' or ''Taniha no Michinushi no Mikoto'') is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from ancient times as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto" or "Tanba Michinushi no Ō," while the ''Kojiki'' uses the name "Tanba Hikotatatsumichi no Ushi no Mikoto" (丹波比古多多須美知能宇斯王).{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
He is the grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] (the 9th Emperor of Japan) and the maternal grandfather of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (the 12th Emperor). As one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, he is said to have been dispatched to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] to aid in its development and pacification.

As one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, he is said to have been dispatched to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] to aid in its development and pacification.

His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 穂国造|1=Hokuni clans of Mikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Uchino Sukune|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}}, and [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumobe Kofun|1=Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215205}} in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Sasayama, Hyōgo]] is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]] enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:道主命 Michinushi no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|spouse={{ill|Q109930105|WD=Q109930105}}|children={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|WD=Q8375821}}; {{ill|Q106114160|WD=Q106114160}}; {{ill|Q108043975|WD=Q108043975}}; {{ill|Q109930164|WD=Q109930164}}; {{ill|Q109930863|WD=Q109930863}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Q11626514|WD=Q11626514}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|WD=Q11466268}}; {{ill|Ōtsutsukitarune no Miko|WD=Q97539311}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11368587]] Wikidata name: Tanba no Michinushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto''' (丹波道主命, ''Tanba no Michinushi no Mikoto'' or ''Taniha no Michinushi no Mikoto'') is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from ancient times as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto" or "Tanba Michinushi no Ō," while the ''Kojiki'' uses the name "Tanba Hikotatatsumichi no Ushi no Mikoto" (丹波比古多多須美知能宇斯王).{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
He is the grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] (the 9th Emperor of Japan) and the maternal grandfather of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (the 12th Emperor). As one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, he is said to have been dispatched to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] to aid in its development and pacification.
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto is the grandson of Emperor Kaika. His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 穂国造|1=Hokuni clans of Mikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Uchino Sukune|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}}, and [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumobe Kofun|1=Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215205}} in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Sasayama, Hyōgo]] is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]] enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}

<!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto--> [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]]<!--standardizing to enwiki standards where this would merely be the intersection of two categories-->[[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:丹波道主命]] {{wikidata link|Q11368587}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:道主命 Michinushi no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|spouse={{ill|Q109930105|WD=Q109930105}}|children={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|WD=Q8375821}}; {{ill|Q106114160|WD=Q106114160}}; {{ill|Q108043975|WD=Q108043975}}; {{ill|Q109930164|WD=Q109930164}}; {{ill|Q109930863|WD=Q109930863}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Q11626514|WD=Q11626514}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|WD=Q11466268}}; {{ill|Ōtsutsukitarune no Miko|WD=Q97539311}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11368587]] Wikidata name: Tanba no Michinushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto''' (丹波道主命, ''Tanba no Michinushi no Mikoto'' or ''Taniha no Michinushi no Mikoto'') is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from ancient times as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto" or "Tanba Michinushi no Ō," while the ''Kojiki'' uses the name "Tanba Hikotatatsumichi no Ushi no Mikoto" (丹波比古多多須美知能宇斯王).{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
He is the grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] (the 9th Emperor of Japan) and the maternal grandfather of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (the 12th Emperor). As one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, he is said to have been dispatched to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] to aid in its development and pacification.
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto is the grandson of Emperor Kaika. His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 穂国造|1=Hokuni clans of Mikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Uchino Sukune|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}}, and [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumobe Kofun|1=Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215205}} in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Sasayama, Hyōgo]] is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]] enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}

According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:道主命 Michinushi no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|spouse={{ill|Q109930105|WD=Q109930105}}|children={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|WD=Q8375821}}; {{ill|Q106114160|WD=Q106114160}}; {{ill|Q108043975|WD=Q108043975}}; {{ill|Q109930164|WD=Q109930164}}; {{ill|Q109930863|WD=Q109930863}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Q11626514|WD=Q11626514}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|WD=Q11466268}}; {{ill|Ōtsutsukitarune no Miko|WD=Q97539311}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11368587]] Wikidata name: Tanba no Michinushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto''' (丹波道主命, ''Tanba no Michinushi no Mikoto'' or ''Taniha no Michinushi no Mikoto'') is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from ancient times as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto" or "Tanba Michinushi no Ō," while the ''Kojiki'' uses the name "Tanba Hikotatatsumichi no Ushi no Mikoto" (丹波比古多多須美知能宇斯王).{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
He is the grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] (the 9th Emperor of Japan) and the maternal grandfather of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (the 12th Emperor). As one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, he is said to have been dispatched to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] to aid in its development and pacification.
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto is the grandson of Emperor Kaika. His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 穂国造|1=Hokuni clans of Mikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Uchino Sukune|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}}, and [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumobe Kofun|1=Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215205}} in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Sasayama, Hyōgo]] is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]] enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|丹波道主命|version=96521673|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Uchino Sukune|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}}, and [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumobe Kofun|1=Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215205}} in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Sasayama, Hyōgo]] is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]] enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|丹波道主命|version=96521673|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:道主命 Michinushi no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|spouse={{ill|Q109930105|WD=Q109930105}}|children={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|WD=Q8375821}}; {{ill|Q106114160|WD=Q106114160}}; {{ill|Q108043975|WD=Q108043975}}; {{ill|Q109930164|WD=Q109930164}}; {{ill|Q109930863|WD=Q109930863}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Q11626514|WD=Q11626514}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|WD=Q11466268}}; {{ill|Ōtsutsukitarune no Miko|WD=Q97539311}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11368587]] Wikidata name: Tanba no Michinushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto''' (丹波道主命, ''Tanba no Michinushi no Mikoto'' or ''Taniha no Michinushi no Mikoto'') is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from ancient times as described in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto" or "Tanba Michinushi no Ō," while the ''Kojiki'' uses the name "Tanba Hikotatatsumichi no Ushi no Mikoto" (丹波比古多多須美知能宇斯王).{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
He is the grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] (the 9th Emperor of Japan) and the maternal grandfather of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (the 12th Emperor). As one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, he is said to have been dispatched to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]] to aid in its development and pacification.
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto is the grandson of Emperor Kaika. His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 穂国造|1=Hokuni clans of Mikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Uchino Sukune|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}}, and [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumobe Kofun|1=Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215205}} in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Sasayama, Hyōgo]] is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]] enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}

The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 穂国造|1=Hokuni clans of Mikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Uchino Sukune|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}}, and [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto]], he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumobe Kofun|1=Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215205}} in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Sasayama, Hyōgo]] is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]] enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|丹波道主命|version=96521673|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:丹波道主命]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:道主命 Michinushi no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|spouse={{ill|Q109930105|WD=Q109930105}}|children={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|WD=Q8375821}}; {{ill|Q106114160|WD=Q106114160}}; {{ill|Q108043975|WD=Q108043975}}; {{ill|Q109930164|WD=Q109930164}}; {{ill|Q109930863|WD=Q109930863}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Q11626514|WD=Q11626514}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|WD=Q11466268}}; {{ill|Ōtsutsukitarune no Miko|WD=Q97539311}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11368587]] Wikidata name: Tanba no Michinushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto''' (丹波道主命, ''Tanba no Michinushi no Mikoto'' or ''Taniha no Michinushi no Mikoto'') is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from ancient times as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}.
The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto" or "Tanba Michinushi no Ō," while the ''Kojiki'' uses the name "Tanba Hikotatatsumichi no Ushi no Mikoto" (丹波比古多多須美知能宇斯王).{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
He is the grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}} (the 9th Emperor of Japan) and the maternal grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} (the 12th Emperor). As one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, he is said to have been dispatched to the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province}} to aid in its development and pacification.
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto is the grandson of Emperor Kaika. His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 穂国造|1=Hokuni clans of Mikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Uchino Sukune|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}}, and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto}}, he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumobe Kofun|1=Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215205}} in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Sasayama, Hyōgo]] is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|qq=}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango}} enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:道主命 Michinushi no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252}}|spouse={{ill|Q109930105|WD=Q109930105}}|children={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|WD=Q8375821}}; {{ill|Q106114160|WD=Q106114160}}; {{ill|Q108043975|WD=Q108043975}}; {{ill|Q109930164|WD=Q109930164}}; {{ill|Q109930863|WD=Q109930863}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Q11626514|WD=Q11626514}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|WD=Q11466268}}; {{ill|Ōtsutsukitarune no Miko|WD=Q97539311}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11368587]] Wikidata name: Tanba no Michinushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto''' (丹波道主命, ''Tanba no Michinushi no Mikoto'' or ''Taniha no Michinushi no Mikoto'') is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from ancient times as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}.
The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto" or "Tanba Michinushi no Ō," while the ''Kojiki'' uses the name "Tanba Hikotatatsumichi no Ushi no Mikoto" (丹波比古多多須美知能宇斯王).{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
He is the grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}} (the 9th Emperor of Japan) and the maternal grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} (the 12th Emperor). As one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, he is said to have been dispatched to the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province}} to aid in its development and pacification.
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto is the grandson of Emperor Kaika. His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 穂国造|1=Hokuni clans of Mikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Uchino Sukune|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}}, and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto}}, he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumobe Kofun|1=Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215205}} in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Sasayama, Hyōgo]] is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|qq=}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango}} enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|丹波道主命|version=96521673|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:丹波道主命]]

According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|丹波道主命|version=96521673|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:丹波道主命]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:丹波道主命]][[zh:丹波道主命]] {{Infobox person|image=File:道主命 Michinushi no mikoto 日本百将伝.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Yamato Kinship|WD=Q2658252|1=Yamato Kingship}}|spouse={{ill|Q109930105|WD=Q109930105|1=Tanba no Kawakami no Masurōjo}}|children={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|WD=Q8375821|1=Hibasu-hime}}; {{ill|Q106114160|WD=Q106114160|1=Chōtei no Wakeno O}}; {{ill|Q108043975|WD=Q108043975|1=Nubataniiri-hime}}; {{ill|Q109930164|WD=Q109930164|1=Madono-hime}}; {{ill|Q109930863|WD=Q109930863|1=Asamihime}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754|1=Saho-hime}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q11626514|WD=Q11626514|1=Ozaho no Ōkimi}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|WD=Q11466268|1=Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka}}; {{ill|Ōtsutsukitarune no Miko|WD=Q97539311|1=Ōtsutsukitarune no Miko}}}} '''Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto''' (丹波道主命, ''Tanba no Michinushi no Mikoto'' or ''Taniha no Michinushi no Mikoto'') is a legendary {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Japanese prince|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese prince|qq=}} from ancient times as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}.
The ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to him as "Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto" or "Tanba Michinushi no Ō," while the ''Kojiki'' uses the name "Tanba Hikotatatsumichi no Ushi no Mikoto" (丹波比古多多須美知能宇斯王).{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
He is the grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}} (the 9th Emperor of Japan) and the maternal grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} (the 12th Emperor). As one of the {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}, he is said to have been dispatched to the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}} to aid in its development and pacification.
== Genealogy == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto is the grandson of Emperor Kaika. His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 穂国造|1=Q11597049|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Uchino Sukune|1=Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}}, and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}}, he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumobe Kofun|1=Kumbe Kurazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215205}} in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Sasayama, Hyōgo]] is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|qq=}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango|1=Kyōtango}} enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|丹波道主命|version=96521673|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:丹波道主命]]

His father is identified as {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoiimasu no Mikoto|qq=}}, a prince of Emperor Kaika, although some sources name {{ill|Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja|彦湯産隅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyumusumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another son of Emperor Kaika, as his father.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki'' provides additional details, naming Hikoiimasu no Mikoto as his father and Okina no Mizuori-hime (息長水依比売娘), the daughter of Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto, as his mother. The ''Kojiki'' also lists his siblings, including Mizunohowaka no Mikoto (ancestor of the {{ill|Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja|近江安直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankoku clans of Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and Kamiookine no Mikoto (ancestor of the Mino Kuni clans).
=== Children === The ''Nihon Shoki'' and ''Kojiki'' list several wives and children of Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, including: * '''Hihausu-hime''' (氷羽州比売命) – Consort of Emperor Suinin and mother of Emperor Keikō. * '''Madonohime''' (真砥野媛) – Consort of Emperor Suinin. * '''Chōtei Wakamiya no Mikoto''' (朝廷別王) – Ancestor of the {{ill|Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja|三川穂国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuni clans of Mikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 穂国造|1=Q11597049|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597049}}.
== Historical Accounts == In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto was sent to Tanba Province in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin’s reign as one of the Four Commanders of the Four Roads. Alongside other commanders such as {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeshi Uchino Sukune|ja|武渟川別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Uchino Sukune|1=Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}}, and {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|WD=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}}, he was instrumental in pacifying the region.{{Sfn|丹波道主命（古代氏族）|2010th year |p=426}}
The ''Kojiki,'' however, attributes the expedition to his father, Hikoiimasu no Mikoto, and offers no further details about his activities.
== Burial == Although the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has not designated an official burial site for Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto, the {{ill|Kumobe Kofun|ja|雲部車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumobe Kofun|1=Kumbe Kurazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215205}} in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Sasayama, Hyōgo]] is traditionally associated with him. This keyhole-shaped tomb, the largest in the Tanba region, is about 140 meters long.{{Sfn|事典陵墓参考地|2005th year |p=49}}
== Legends == The {{ill|Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya|qq=}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango|1=Kyōtango}} enshrines Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto as its deity. According to legend, he wielded a sacred sword called "Kunimi no Ken," which gave rise to place names such as "Kunimi" and "Kumihama."{{Sfn|京丹後市観光協会|2005th year }}
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Four Commanders of the Four Roads|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Commanders of the Four Roads|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}}
